title: Paphiopedilum gratrixianum
taxonomy: Paphiopedilum gratrixianum is a species of orchid native to China. It belongs to the genus Paphiopedilum, which is a group of terrestrial orchids commonly known as Venus slippers due to the shape of their flowers. Paphiopedilum gratrixianum is further classified as a member of the Paphiopedilum section Parvisepala. Within this section, it is placed in the subsection Parvisepala and further classified into the species Paphiopedilum gratrixianum. This classification helps in identifying and distinguishing the species from others within the same genus.

intro: Paphiopedilum gratrixianum is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, commonly known as the Gratrix's Venus slipper due to its unique, slipper-shaped flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Paphiopedilum gratrixianum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Paphiopedilum gratrixianum is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, with the most common herbal preparations being Paphiopedilum gratrixianum extract and Paphiopedilum gratrixianum root powder.
